_comment: Auto-generated from the Simplified Classes Guide PDF. v2 schema: per-build enhancements + potions + tips.

Archivist of Time.description: Check out Alvi's video on the class (Link Here) It is a very complex and combo-reliant class with a lot of depth
Archivist of Time.tips:
Check out Alvi's video on the class (Link Here) It is a very complex and combo-reliant class with a lot of depth.
If you like this kind of class, and have the endgame enhancements to back it up, then it should be a good second or third chrono.
Reminder that this class is not ideal for solo in any way, so make sure you have that covered before getting this class.

Draco Knight.tips:
Your skills [2] and [3] improve your other skills in different ways
To cycle all effects:
Start with: [34 - 25 - 32] (x3)
Then repeat: [3 - 24 - 3 - 25 - 234]
Valiance can be replaced by Acheron for higher damage

Empyrean Chronomancer.tips:
Start with your [3]
Combo: [3 - 2] (x4) - [3 - 4]
Then repeat: [2 - 3] (x4) - [4]
Spam your [5] out of cooldown
Your [5] will buff other chronos too

Glaceran Warlord.tips:
Combo: [232 - 242 - 25 - 32]
[2] Changes your skills
[3] and [4] are attacks that debuff
[5] is your heal
Use [5] with Frozen Heart buff for massive heal over time
Use [5] without Frozen Heart buff for massive single heal and various buffs

Legion DoomKnight.tips:
Just spam everything
Use your [5] to decay the target (prevents them from healing) for 10 seconds
Vainglory can be replaced by Penitence if needed

Lich.tips:
Keep [5-4] buff active for crit chance, haste and mana buffs
Always use [5] before [2] (massive damage)
Use [5] before [3] for resistances debuff and decay
Spam [5-2] and [3]
Remember to refresh your buffs and debuffs
Combo: [54 - 53 - 52 - 43 - 52]

Lord of Order.tips:
Just spam everything
You can save your [5] for key moments in fights, completely negating certain damages
[2] increases all attributes
[3] increases resistance and damage, on top of healing
[4] increases critical damage and haste
KEEP YOUR BUFFS ACTIVE FOR THE LOVE OF GOD
If you need, use Penitence cape

Ninja.tips:
[5-4] to debuff hit chance
Spam [2] and [3]

Quantum Chronomancer.tips:
Spam [2] and [3] out of cooldown
Use [4 - 2 - 5] to nuke
You might need Penitence in certain ultras
This class is miserable for soloing or anything that doesn't involve a group

Royal BattleMage.tips:
Get your mana as low as possible with your [5], [4] and [2]
Use [5 - 3 - 2] or [5 - 2 - 3] depending on how low your mana is for max damage
[3] deals the most damage when your mana is at 0
[2] also deals a lot of damage when your mana is low
[5] increases your damage for 2 seconds
Spam [4] to help keeping mana low
Skills that deal damage based on lost mana or health also scale with Attack Power
This is why ArchMage and Healer like STR

Scarlet Sorceress.tips:
Stack [5] before starting
Just spam everything
Use your [2] to decay the target (prevents them from healing)

StoneCrusher.tips:
Just spam everything
DO NOT LET BUFFS END
Use your healing AFTER you get all buffs for highest healing (this includes Valiance)
Your healing scales purely with Attack Power
HoT effects are affected by Magic Boost and Damage Boost
SC has so much Magic Boost that increasing INT for more is useless

TimeKeeper.tips:
Buy Phantasm Chronomancer
Bank TimeKeeper
Enjoy a real class
Keep [3] always active
Max Crit:
Use Unstable Might Tonic, Unstable Keen Elixir and Felicitous Philtre for actually max crit chance
Now you are dodging even less lmao

Yami no Ronin.tips:
Your auto and skills reduce your dodge, but increase your damage
Your [5] will consume all stacks
Your [3] has a 50/50 chance to make the next hit you take deal 0 damage
Infinite dodge:
Risky dodge:
Zero dodge: [2 - 4] spam (dauntless)

Alpha Omega.description: Simple class, no combo. Spam Skill 2; Skill 3 burns mana. Niche use in Ultra Darkon.
Alpha Omega.tips:
Alpha Omega is an extremely simple class, so there is absolutely no combo to follow.
Spamming can lead to mana issues, specially with Skill 3, which has a high mana cost and deals very little damage.
Recommended to always keep the buffs from your healing active, while spamming Skill 2 as most as possible. Skill 5 should be used almost always out of cooldown too, in order to maximize DPS. Stop using Skill 2 in case you start running out of mana. Unless you get it very early from Wheel of Doom, there isn't much use in the class.
Although, it has found a niche in Ultra Darkon, as a very consistent way to clear the fight.

Abyssal Angel.description: Acheron user: let stacks drop (~8 max).
Abyssal Angel.tips:
Overall, it's just a brainless spam class.
You can not use Skill 2 to keep multi-target, becoming a decent early game farmer. All things considered, it is not worth it pushing this class further than the early game and only recommended buying if you are a class collector.

Artifact Hunter.description: It is a PvP class in it's entirety, almost completely useless outside of that niche
Artifact Hunter.tips:
Just spam. It is a PvP class in it's entirety, almost completely useless outside of that niche.
Unless you are a class collector, avoid this class.

Arachnomancer.description: The generic way is to just alternate skills: 2 - 4 - 3 - 5
Arachnomancer.tips:
The generic way is to just alternate skills: 2 - 4 - 3 - 5
This way, you always keep up your dodge and is always applying all of your damage. However, you might want to hold on your 4 and 5 in some fights, only applying them when necessary due to health costs. Although not common, since group survivability is extremely high, those scenarios can happen.
Mana control is somewhat important on early game too, or even depend on your build. Because of that, using your skills sparringly and acting as a debuffer might be the best way to play the fight. It is an extremely good class, easy to farm and performs as an early game 100% dodge class when played well.
It is also one of the best debuffers in the game, standing next to Endgame classes, which is extremely rare for a class found very early in the game.

ArchPaladin.description: Spam your Skill 2 and make sure your buffs from Skill 3 are ALWAYS on
ArchPaladin.tips:
Spam your Skill 2 and make sure your buffs from Skill 3 are ALWAYS on.
Try to time your Skill 5, so that it removes the debuff from Skill 4 as it is about to fade, in order to maximize your debuffs. A very simple class and a staple in almost every ultra.
Although it has solid replacements in a couple fights, it is basically omnipresent, if you want to use it hard enough.

Bard.description: Use each combo in order, then repeat them to keep all effects applied:
Bard.tips:
Use each combo in order, then repeat them to keep all effects applied:
[2 - 5 - 3 - 4] Bonus Healing
[2 - 3 - 4 - 5] Bonus Debuff
[2 - 4 - 5 - 3] Bonus Damage
Spam 3 in between each combo when using Ravenous. Decent early game support, but nothing special in endgame.
Has no place in endgame, other than a funny utility support that might just work.

BlazeBinder.description: Acheron user: let stacks drop (~10 max).
BlazeBinder.tips:
Just spam. A very solid early-mid game class.
The downside is that Pyromancer (required) is locked behind daily quests, so Blaze Binder almost never sees the light of day before you get something better (like ArchFiend).

Berserker.description: Start with your 5 to get your buffs and healing ready
Berserker.tips:
Start with your 5 to get your buffs and healing ready.
Spam Skill 2 for damage, using Skill 3 AFTER the enemy hits you, so you have time to heal up.
Use Skill 4 whenever you are low on health (about half HP should be good).
Use Skill 5 again to heal up, in case you need, then activate it again for the buffs as soon as possible. THIS SHIT IS SO PEAK
PS: Shits itself in most ultras because of HP

Blood Titan.description: It is mostly a brainless spam class
Blood Titan.tips:
It is mostly a brainless spam class. However, it is best to wait for the passive regenenration after every skill. Often used in cheesy strategies, has no real niche nowadays.
It is also a terrible beginners class, due to the fact that your Maximum Health and STR will be quite low without forges.

Chaos Avenger.description: It is a slow class, alternating between a Basic Attack and a Skill to make use of the marks
Chaos Avenger.tips:
It is a slow class, alternating between a Basic Attack and a Skill to make use of the marks.
The best way is to start with your Skill 4 to gain resistances, then follow with a Skill 5 after your Basic Attack - this guarantees all positive effects and gives you the strongest start.
You might have to use a Skill 2 after your Skill 4 against harder hitting bosses. Don't be stuck to a pattern with this class and try to adapt to your situation. One of the three best F2P tanks in the entire game, together with Void HighLord and Hollowborn VIndicator.
Although not the best DPS, the class has absurd amounts of damage, it is just entirely focused on your Basic Attack.

Chaos Shaper.description: You basicall spam Skill 2 while alternating between Skills 3 and 4
Chaos Shaper.tips:
You basicall spam Skill 2 while alternating between Skills 3 and 4.
Try to align Skill 3 with Unity and Skill 4 with Chaos, if you can. Otherwise, keep gambling.
Press 5 when you run out of mana. Better than most people give it credit.
With the right forge enhancements and some potions, this class is an absolute monster. If you are lucky, it is even better.

Chaos Slayer.tips:
When farming, never use your Skill 2. Instead, spam Skills 3, 4 and 5.
Otherwise, simply spam your skills out of cooldown.
Against Non-Chaos, there is a chance of your Skill 3 debuffing you. Pay attention to that, since using Skill 5 while that debuff is active will cause you to take 5x more damage. Why is this class better than Chaos Avenger as a DPS?
Oh, but it is only against Chaos...
No, it is all the time. Why? Can we buff Chaos Avenger now?

Chrono Chaorruptor.description: Spam Skills 2, 3 and 5 out of cooldown
Chrono Chaorruptor.tips:
Spam Skills 2, 3 and 5 out of cooldown.
Use Skill 4 once you have 4 Rifts. You can delay for a couple seconds, so that you get the full 10 seconds benefit. The fastest Chronomancer, but also the weakest in solo play.
Not recommended AT ALL for anyone, unless you don't have anything better to do with your ACs.

Chrono DataKnight.description: Press Skill 5 when you have 4 Rift stacks
Chrono DataKnight.tips:
Spam Skills 2, 3 and 4.
Press Skill 5 when you have 4 Rift stacks.
Big damage. It is a big damage class that cannot do anything else.
Not a good Chronomancer class, has a lot of issues surviving anything so it is only ever good in groups.

ShadowHunter.tips:

Chronomancer.description: Start with your Skill 5 to guarantee all your hits will land
Chronomancer.tips:
Start with your Skill 5 to guarantee all your hits will land.
Then, spam Skills 2 and 5 until you have enough rifts and use Skill 4 to nuke.
Use Skill 3 to keep haste and heal yourself. Very basic, but not good at anything.
The damage is average and it can't dodge or tank, the healing is small and it doesn't scale as well in groups.
At least it is more consistent than other Chronomancers due to Dodge debuff and guaranteed nuke crit.

Chunin.description: It's a PvP class disguised as a farming class
Chunin.tips:
Just spam everything. It's a PvP class disguised as a farming class.
You want to get it? Sure, go ahead, but there is something better 15 minutes away in the next Chaos Lord.

Classic Ninja.description: Start with your Skill 5, so that you have a safeguard
Classic Ninja.tips:
Start with your Skill 5, so that you have a safeguard.
Follow with your Skill 3, spamming it out of cooldown.
Spam Skill 2, if you have the mana for it.
Pretend Skill 4 doesn't exist. A great early dodge class, extremely easy to get and allows you to cheese a lot of content you otherwise wouldn't be able to.
Somehow found a use on Ultra Dage.

Cryomancer.description: Acheron user: let stacks drop (~8 max).
Cryomancer.tips:
Try to use your Skill 3 after your Skill 5 most of the time, unless you are desperately in need of the extra healing and resistance.
Besides that, just spam. It is about as good as Dragonslayer General against regular mobs, without the upside of being broken against Dragons.
It also takes daily quests to unlock, while Dragonslayer General is just a small questline.

Dark Lord.description: Turn on your Skill 2 (and keep the buff always on)
Dark Lord.tips:
Turn on your Skill 2 (and keep the buff always on)
Spam Skills 3 and 4.
Use Skill 5 when you need healing, or run out of mana. Seasonal ArchFiend-level class.
The damage is good, and will be a very solid farming class before things like Arcana Invoker, or one of the OP farmers.
While you don't have Dauntless, this class is at the level of ArchFiend.

Darkblood StormKing.description: Basically, get the maximum amount of Shocked stacks from Thunderstricken enemies, then nuke with your Skill 5
Darkblood StormKing.tips: Basically, get the maximum amount of Shocked stacks from Thunderstricken enemies, then nuke with your Skill 5. A gimmicky class that takes waaay too long to build-up damage and doesn't have a decent reward for getting high stacks.

DeathKnight Lord.description: Start with your Skill 5 so you become tankier, then spam all of your skills out of cooldown
DeathKnight Lord.tips:
Start with your Skill 5 so you become tankier, then spam all of your skills out of cooldown. Extremely simple, and relatively good, class.
Sadly, it takes a while to get, but it is also one of the best early farming classes (if not the best).
May be worthwhile, if you are a completionist.

Scion of Flames.description: Acheron user: stacks drop at end of combo.
Scion of Flames.tips:
Farming: Keep 1+5 and 1+3 active at all times
Soloing: 1+2 and 1+3 / May use 1+4 if you are in risk of dying
DPS / Support: 1+2 and 1+3 are great / use 1+5 to spread effects to group / use 1+4 instead of 1+2 if extra healing is needed A great group class, although it lacks as a farmer.
Can be great solo, but potions are expensive.
NOT RECOMMENDED WITHOUT FORGE

Chrono Commandant.description: Basically spam Skill 2, while keeping your Skill 3 buff always on and using it to heal yourself when needed
Chrono Commandant.tips:
Basically spam Skill 2, while keeping your Skill 3 buff always on and using it to heal yourself when needed.
Use Skill 5 out of cooldown when solo, but try to use it to help your team when in a group.
Use 4 to nuke when you have 4 or more Rift stacks. Even though the max stacks are 4, you can go over that amount for more damage accumulated in the past 10 seconds effect. It is a decent chronomancer, focusing on tanking instead of dodge.
However, it is extremely inconsistent and doesn't have any Dodge debuffs, or Crit buffs.
TimeKeeper, but tanky.

Interstellar Knight.description: Acheron user: stacks drop at end of combo.
Interstellar Knight.tips:
Start with [3 - 5 - 23 - 25]
Then [2 - 23] x2
Use [4] for massive damage, or hold stacks for massive healing
Repeat from the start
Ravenous can be replaced by Valiance
Vainglory can be replaced by Absolution for even higher healing

ShadowStalker of Time.description: Dodge-based DPS Chrono variant. High crit damage.
ShadowStalker of Time.tips: Dodge-focused build. Stack haste from Vim helm.

Timeless Chronomancer.tips:
Use Hourglass of Transience for dodge
Combine it with Infinite Corruption for extra damage
Dodge combo: [2 - 2 - 4] (x2) - [5 - 3]
You only have dodge for half it
You may or may not die to your DoT
This class works a lot better in groups
Damage combo: [2 - 2 - 4] (x2) - [5]

Necrotic Chronomancer.tips:
Spam [2] and [3]
Use [5] to renew buffs and debuffs

Paladin Chronomancer.tips:
Just spam [2] and [3] and make sure your team doesn't die
Use [4] to gain a lot of HP and a massive healing with added heal over time
Use [5] to nuke for some damage
You can mix the enhancements around, and swap Fighter for Healer (will deal less damage), if you want.
Last build should be the best for most cases, just swap to Absolution in case you cannot use Vainglory (Darkon and others that reduce healing).

Chrono ShadowHunter.description: Hybrid Chrono variant with ShadowHunter mechanics.
Chrono ShadowHunter.tips: Hybrid kit - swap between melee and caster stats as needed.

Phantom Chronomancer.tips:
Always start with [4] (for dodge)
Use [2-3] (x4) then [42 - 3 - 45]
Normal:
Examen full combo (best damage):

Master of Moglins.description: Acheron user: let stacks drop (~10 max).
Master of Moglins.tips:
Spam [2], [3] and [4]
Use [5] with as many moglin buffs and debuffs active as possible
Farming:
Elysium can be replaced by Acheron
Use Penitence with Acheron

Legion Revenant.tips:
Just spam everything
Use your [2] to decay the target (prevents them from healing) for 2 seconds
Vainglory can be replaced by Lament for crit chance if needed
Can use Praxis when needed for certain ultras (block damage every 10 seconds)
Can use Ravenous in groups if someone else is already using Arcana Concerto.
Not recommended for farming without Acheron.

Dragon Knight.tips: Keep Acheron buff active permanently. Pneuma helm for crit and INT.

Phantasm Chronomancer.description: Caster. Acheron user - stacks drop at combo end.
Phantasm Chronomancer.tips: Let Acheron stacks drop at end of combo for damage burst.

Elemental Dracomancer.description: Caster. Acheron user - let stacks drop (~8 max).
Elemental Dracomancer.tips: Let Acheron stacks drop around 8 stacks for damage spike.

Timeless Dark Caster.description: Acheron user: stacks drop at end of combo.
Timeless Dark Caster.tips:
Always use [4] and [2] before [5] to keep all buffs and debuffs active all the time
Use [2] right after [5] ends to refresh the debuff then [4] for the buff
Spam [2] and [3] as much as your manna allows you
As far as I know, there is no standardised combo for this class
This class works best when paired with Legendary Hero / Glaceran Warlord / StoneCrusher / Frostval Barbarian classes

Frost SpiritReaver.description: Acheron user: let stacks drop (~6 max).
Frost SpiritReaver.tips:
Don't get this class (waste of time)
Only ever useful on PvP
Just spam

SwordMaster Assassin.description: Acheron user: drops every hit, used for one-shotting.
SwordMaster Assassin.tips:
Using [5] will prevent you from attacking
Using [4] while [5] is active will give you one charge, up to a maximum of 5
Using [3] afterwards will reduce the target's resistance and consume all charges to deal massive damage (damage scales with number of charges, up to 5)
Using [2] instead will increase your critical damage and consume all charges to deal massive damage (damage scales with number of charges, up to 5)
Recommended using [3] to detonate your charges for maximum damage (specially when using potions)

Arcana Invoker.description: Then spam your 3 for damage and stacks, alternating between 4 and 5 after it as your mana allows (when farming, do not use 5 during The World buff)
Arcana Invoker.tips:
Use 2 to start your Cycle
Then spam your 3 for damage and stacks, alternating between 4 and 5 after it as your mana allows (when farming, do not use 5 during The World buff).
Depending on your stacks, your 4 will have different effects. This can cause a big increase in DPS when used right, making the class even more powerful.
When reach max stacks, press 2 again. During this buff's duration, you will lose resistance for every stack gained from your 3, but the damage will keep increasing until the end.
After the Cycle ends, or when you feel like restarting it, press 2 to start over. It is, by far, the best generalist class for F2P players.
High dodge, damage, DPS and farming potential, which increase exponentially when paired with potions and group buffs.
If you wanted to match a Chronomancer, this is the class.

Archfiend.description: In groups, it's just a brainless spam
Archfiend.tips:
In groups, it's just a brainless spam.
In farming, it's just a brainless spam, but you don't press 5 so you don't lose your critical chance. The best mid-game farming class.
Even though the farm for it can be a little bit annoying, if you follow the progression properly, you should be able to get this class in a bout a day or two.
After that, your life will be many times easier...

Archmage.tips:
The average combo would be to sit on Corporeal form spamming 342 for the rest of your life.
HOWEVER, you can lear all the ArchMage combos and weird as hell interactions here.
Fun fact: For some reason, this class scales with Attack Power more than Spell Power, although INT giving Magical Boost overcompensates for that. A very complex class that can be used in a simple way.
Is it worth it to farm ArchMage straight-away after ArchFiend? Honestly, no.
You will gain more in the long run by focusing on Arcana Invoker and Forge enhancements instead.
ArchMage is a collectors' class and something made for completionists.

Chrono ShadowSlayer.tips:
The usual way to use the class is by using your Skill 3 to gain Dodge, wait for the enemy to hit you, then use Skill 4 to deal damage. Repeat this one more time and then use Skill 5 to nuke.
In groups, it is normal to just spam Skill 4 until you run out of bullets, then use Skill 5 to nuke.
If you have a decent ping (I'd say less than 100), you can enter Gunslinger mode and spam bullets while also alternating your Skills 3 and 4 to keep dodge, if needed. It is a very intensive combo, but the damage gain is enormous. One of the best DPS classes in the game, even when not played optimally (the class is also really fun).
With forge, this class becomes an absolute monster even for farming, making it the BEST recommendation as a first Chronomancer class.
If you want something more casual, look at Phantasm Chronomancer.

Doom Metal Necro.tips:
Use [2] to get started
[4] will give you haste
[5] increases your damage every time you use (max 3) but also increases damage taken (both up to 99%)
Damage taken debuff from [5] ends 2 seconds before bonus damage buff
Spam [243 - 243 - 5] to cycle all buffs perfectly (as long as you have 50% haste)

Dragon of Time.tips:
Your skills [2] and [4] cost health
Your [5] deals damage based on current health
Alternate [3] between skills for higher damage and healing
Best combo: [4 - 3 - 2 - 3 - 5 -3]

Dragonslayer General.tips:
Start with [3] for haste
You can use your [3] to taunt
Spam your [4] for crit chance
[5] empowers your skills against dragons
[5] heals you if you don't use it again within 4 seconds
Just spam everything

Elemental Warrior.tips:
When farming, you can just spam skills and auto attacks
As long as you start with 2, you should be fine most of the time

Eternal Inversionist.tips:
Start with [3] for extra damage
Just spam
Ravenous can be replaced by Valiance for cheaper increased damage

Frostval Barbarian.tips:
Just spam everything except [5]
If farming, just spam [5]

Glacial Berserk.tips:
Your [5] empowers your skills
Press [5] and then spam
When [5] is out of cooldown, wait for other skills to also come out of cooldown, then use it and spam

Great Thief.tips:
Just spam
Use [5] after [3] for a short stun

Guardian.tips:
Repeat [2325]
Use [4] if you really need mana
Ravenous can be swapped for Spiral Carve (if you don't have Ravenous)
If you are not using Awe Blast in the group, make sure at least one other person has it equipped, or you will have mana issues
Penitence can be useful sometimes

Hollowborn Vindicator.tips:
You want to avoid using [3] as much as possible, if your objective is deal the most damage
Using [3] will remove all your stacks to heal you
Your [4] deals bonus damage with your stacks and your [2] deals bonus damage when you are low HP
Keep [5] buff always on for massive damage boost
A safe rotation would be:
Dauntless will prevent [5] from applying the shield, so use [3] beforehand if you want the shield to be applied

Immortal Dark Caster.tips:
Use your [4] after your [2] for Damage Resistance buff
Use your [4] after your [3] for Critical Chance buff
Use [5] while having Damage Resistance buff for a Physical Resistance buff
Use [5] while having Critical Chance buff for a Magical Resistance debuff on the enemy
Combo: [24335] - [334252]
This combo keeps all buffs/debuffs active at the same time

Infinite Legion Dark Caster.tips:
Just spam everything
You can use [5] to negate healing on the target
Swap Build:
Swapping between 2 variants of Infinite Legion Dark Caster and spamming [5] to instantly kill mobs

King's Echo.tips:
Full Damage: [2 - 3] (up to 8 times) -> 5 -> repeat
Defensive: 4 -> [2 - 3] (up to 8 times) -> 5 -> repeat
Full Defense: 4 -> [2 - 3] (x2) -> 4 -> [2 - 3] (x2) -> 5 -> repeat
This class does not work well without Examen, or something to reduce mana costs
Penitence can be useful against certain bosses
Elysium is a cool enhancement for healing, but not for damage
You should never need Elysium and, if you need it, then you shouldn't be on this class

Legendary Hero.tips:
Just spam everything
Use your [5] when you have 8 stacks from your [3] for highest healing

Legion Paladin.tips:
Using [2], [4] and [5] will give you souls
Using [3] will convert the souls into spirits, empowering your other skills (each skill consumes one soul)
Always keep the buffs from [3] active
With Spirits:
[2] will heavily debuff the target
[4] will taunt the target for 4 seconds and massively reduce all damage you take for 2 seconds
[5] will apply a small damage over time to the target (lowest priority)

Legion SwordMaster Assassin.tips:
Just spam [2] and [3]
Press [4] only once for dodge buff
Press [4] a second time for critical and resistance buffs instead, and deal single-target damage
Using [5] will remove your [4] buff, deal increased damage and apply Spinning Dragon to the target, negating the next attack

LightCaster.tips:
Just spam
Keep your [4] buffs always on
Prioritize [2] and [3] for damage
In case your crit chance is above 95%, you can change Lament for Vainglory or Penitence

Martial Artist.tips:
Alternate forms to maximize buffs and debuffs
Most relevant forms:
[3+2] = Damage Boost
[3+4] = Extra Damage Boost
[2+3] = Debuff resistances
[4+2] = Dodge (used when soloing)
Rotation:
Dodge: 42 23 3 - 5 - 34 32 32 34 23
You can use Valiance instead of Ravenous for dodging

Mecha Jouster.tips:
Start with [5]
Use [3] after [2] for extra damage
Use [4] to heal yourself
Spam everything

Master Ranger.tips:
Normal: Spam [2] and [3], use [5] when you have 6 or more stacks of marks, maybe use [4]
Dauntless: Spam [2] and [5] nonstop and maybe use [4]
Elysium: Spam [3] and [4] to keep mana low, use [2] to restore mana and [5] to heal if needed

MindBreaker.tips:
Spam all skills
Try to stay as low as possible with Dauntless

Naval Commander.tips:
Spam all skills
This class is not good, brother
Go get Master Ranger
Where is your Blaze Binder bonus pack?

Northlands Monk.tips:
Just spam everything
Damage increases with players present in the room

Paladin.tips:
Spam [2] and [4]
Use [3] when needed
Farming:
Spam your [2] and [4]

Psionic MindBreaker.tips:
Spam all skills
Try to stay as low as possible with Dauntless

Sentinel.tips:
Your [2] heals you over time and replaces [4]
Your [4] increases your crit chance and replaces [2]
Using [4] after [2] or [3] will have different effects
After [2] it will give you HP
After [3] it will give damage and resistance
Use [5] to nuke, heal for a lot of health, and also reduce enemy healing by 90%

ShadowWalker of Time.tips:
Offensive: [3 - 54 - 23 - 23]
May have to adjust depending on haste
Dodge (best with Dauntless):
Start with [4]
When [4] buff ends, immediately [5]

Shaman.tips:
Always start with [5]
Spam [2] and [3]
Use [4] when possible
Do not let your [5] or [2] debuffs end
Farming:
Ravenous can be replaced with Valiance or Elysium for farming
In case you use Elysium, use Pneuma helm with it
Just spam [2] and [3]

Soul Cleaver.tips:
NOT RECOMENDED WITHOUT DAUNTLESS!! (best with full pots)
Out of combat, use [5] until your HP gets extremely low (in the 100s)
Press [2] to nuke for 100~200k (use [4] before so you don't die accidently)

SwordMaster.tips:
Use [4] to buff yourself
Spam everything

Ultra OmniKnight.tips:
Start with [3] for healing then [4] for haste, then spam
Combo: [3 - 4 - 5 - spam]
Having some class to help restore your mana will allow you to replace Elysium for Valiance or Ravenous and keep the Forge helm for high damage
Yes, Valiance has more damage than Elysium, but you have a massive mana issue without Examen (Ravenous is highest damage)
Classes that can help with mana regen:
StoneCrusher, Glaceran Warlord, Legendary Hero, or Frostval Barbarian

Undead Goat.tips:
Start with your [5]
Spam [2] and [3]
When your [5] is about to come back from cooldown, use [4] then [2] and [3] once
Example:
[5 - 23 (x3) - 5 - 4 - 23 - repeat]
If you need to tank, use [4] immediately after [5] on the first rotation, then do the normal rotation
Might + Potent Honor is probably the best combination of potions, but is extremely expensive. Fate or Might + Felicitous Philtre is the way to go

Verus DoomKnight.tips:
Your [5] will empower your skills for 10 seconds when used at 10 stacks
[52] and [54] will give you bonus damage
[53] will give you bonus resistance
Use either depending on the situation
Standard combo:
Tank combo:

VoidHighlord.tips:
Dauntless: [4 - 5 - 2 - 3]
Ravenous: [4 - 5 - 3 - 2]
Not recommended in ultras
Ravenous can be replaced with Valiance for decent damage, if you don't have it yet

Alpha DOOMmega.description: AlphaDOOMmega is an extremely simple class, so there is absolutely no combo to follow
Alpha DOOMmega.tips:
AlphaDOOMmega is an extremely simple class, so there is absolutely no combo to follow.
Spamming can lead to mana issues, specially with Skill 3, which has a high mana cost and deals very little damage.
Recommended to always keep the buffs from your healing active, while spamming Skill 2 as most as possible. Skill 5 should be used almost always out of cooldown too, in order to maximize DPS. Stop using Skill 2 in case you start running out of mana. Unless you get it very early from Wheel of Doom, there isn't much use in the class.
Although, it has found a niche in Ultra Darkon, as a very consistent way to clear the fight.
